prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_rf', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.24) and high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA excels at both trend tracking (0.9435 seas_corr) and seasonality, while THETA dominates overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf/CWT_rf/DWT_svr), supporting per-horizon selection. Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without overfitting to any single pattern.

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.24) and high seasonality variance (0.96), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA excels at both trend tracking (0.9435 seas_corr) and seasonality, while THETA dominates overall RMSE. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_rf/CWT_rf/DWT_svr), supporting per-horizon selection. Given the high disagreement and variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.96) and model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.24). n_unique_winners=9 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring best_per_horizon selection. With only n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 on inverse_rmse_weights prevents overfitting. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 adds diversity with small k to capture early-horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) separately from late-horizon models.
